commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
I am. Hebrew, "He hath cast me into the mire "(Protestants) or, "He regards me as dirt; my portion is on the earth and dust. "(Haydock)
All look upon me with horror and contempt. (Calmet)

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 60. Since in the judgment of lost sinners the Holy Church of the Elect is ‘likened to mire,’ because it is trodden under and despised in time; it is ‘compared to dust and ashes,’ because while they do not see her interior good things, they calculate that she has been brought to those bad things alone, which they see brought to view in her in the body. But the faithful people of the Elect, while it sees many fall from itself, wishes, if it might obtain this, to have the times of its adversities reversed. Because the prayer of this people is delayed, its groaning is increased.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
